mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation erodes real corporate earnings and increases discount rates, directly reducing equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year IC for CPIAUCSL as single predictor of 20-day returns is not significantly negative (t-stat < -1.96) or positive in any significant portion.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 3.25%, labor markets become overheated, leading to wage inflation and margin compression that negatively impacts equities over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE <= 3.25%, or average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and expansion in retail sales signals growing supply-demand imbalance, creating inflationary pressure that hurts equity valuations over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline for at least 2 months and RSAFS month-over-month growth > 1%
failure_condition: The interaction term in a regression of 60-day returns on INDPRO and RSAFS changes is not significantly negative (p < 0.05) over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions with GDPC1 annualized growth above 2%, rising housing starts reflect robust demand and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: In expansion regimes, HOUST positive changes have a Rank IC < 0.05 for 20-day returns over any 3-year window, or interactive test shows no significant predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales and food services signals strong consumer demand, which supports corporate revenues and drives positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year IC for RSAFS as predictor of 20-day returns is not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.96) or is negative in any significant portion.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1.5 million units monthly, it signals above-trend construction activity that may stretch supply chains and lead to resurgent inflation, negatively impacting equities over a quarter.
failure_condition: Average S&P 500 60-day return following HOUST > 1500 is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and decreases in the unemployment rate signal aggressive tightening amid a strong labor market, which increases recession risk and depresses equity prices over one month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25bp in the last 3 months and UNRATE declines by at least 0.2pp
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient in a linear model predicting 20-day returns from FEDFUNDS change and UNRATE change is not significantly negative (p < 0.10) over rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime (GDPC1 positive growth following a contraction), falling unemployment initially boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery (GDPC1 positive growth after contraction)
failure_condition: In post-recession recovery periods, the IC for UNRATE change on 20-day returns is not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.96) or the signal becomes negative in more than 30% of such periods.
